heading: Procurement of services: contracts for professional and technical services
label: 4507 Procurement of services: contracts for professional and technical services
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 4507, , , which related to sale of ordnance and ordnance stores to designers, was repealed by , , .act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 104170A Stat. 253Pub. L. 103–160, div. A, title VIII, § 822(b)(3)107 Stat. 1706Nov. 30, 1993

Amendments2021— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 116–283section 2331 of this title2001— substituted “Procurement of services: contracts” for “Contracts” in section catchline.Pub. L. 107–1071994—Subsec. (c). struck out text and heading of subsec. (c). Text read as follows:Pub. L. 103–355“(1) The Secretary of Defense may waive the limitation in on the total value of task orders for specific contracting activities to the extent the Secretary considers the use of master agreements necessary in order to further the policy set forth in subsection (a).section 2304(j)(4) of this title“(2) During any fiscal year, such a waiver may not increase the total value of task orders under master agreements of a contracting activity by more than 20 percent of the value of all contracts for advisory and assistance services awarded by that contracting activity during fiscal year 1989.“(3) Such a waiver shall not become effective until 60 days after the Secretary of Defense has published notice thereof in the Federal Register.”1991—Subsec. (c)(1). struck out “on a case-by-case basis” after “value of task orders”, substituted “considers the use of master agreements necessary” for “considers necessary the use of master agreements”, and struck out “of this section” before period at end.Pub. L. 102–25

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall prescribe regulations to ensure, to the maximum extent practicable, that professional and technical services are acquired on the basis of the task to be performed rather than on the basis of the number of hours of services provided.
include standards and approval procedures to minimize the use of such contracts;
establish criteria to ensure that proposals for contracts for technical and professional services are evaluated on a basis which does not encourage contractors to propose uncompensated overtime;
ensure appropriate emphasis on technical and quality factors in the source selection process;
require identification of any hours in excess of 40-hour weeks included in a proposal;

ensure that offerors are notified that proposals which include unrealistically low labor rates or which do not otherwise demonstrate cost realism will be considered in a risk assessment and evaluated appropriately; and
provide guidance to contracting officers to ensure that any use of uncompensated overtime will not degrade the level of technical expertise required to perform the contract.
